subject: Ecology
title: Whale Falls
description: Learn Whale Falls in one minute.

hook.text: When a whale dies and sinks, its carcass becomes an island of life on the barren seafloor.

buildup.text: Scavengers arrive first—hagfish, sleeper sharks, crabs—stripping flesh in months.

discovery.text: Bacteria then colonize the bones, digesting oils and fats for years.

twist.text: A single whale fall can support an entire community for 50 to 100 years.

climax.text: Dozens of species found at whale falls exist nowhere else on Earth.

punchline.text: In death, a whale feeds a century of life.

quiz.question: How long can a whale fall support a deep-sea community?
